title: OrganiGram Holdings (OGI) Q4 2020 Earnings Call Summary
text: OrganiGram Holdings Q4 2020 Earnings Summary. Financial Performance: OrganiGram reported gross revenue of $25.4 million in Q4 2020, representing 32% growth compared to the prior year. Net revenue grew 25% to $20.4 million. For the full fiscal year, gross revenue reached $103.4 million with approximately $87 million in net revenue. The company achieved positive adjusted EBITDA for the second consecutive year, though the quarter showed adjusted EBITDA of negative $2.7 million. Operational Highlights - Product Portfolio Expansion: Management launched 40 new SKUs since July, including novel offerings across dried flower, edibles, vapes, and beverages. An additional 18 SKUs were planned for Q2 fiscal 2021. International Growth: The company made its first shipment to Israel under an exclusive supply agreement with Canndoc, representing OrganiGram's largest international deal to date. Strategic Investment: Following successful milestone completion, OrganiGram invested an additional $2.5 million in Hyasynth Biologicals, bringing total investment to $7.5 million. Key Challenges: Management cited production inefficiencies persisting during portfolio revitalization. An $11.1 million noncash inventory provision reflected excess trim, concentrates, and net realizable value adjustments. Staffing constraints led to unfulfilled purchase orders, particularly in Ontario's competitive market. Market Context: The Canadian adult-use market reached an annualized run rate of $3.1 billion by September 2020, nearly doubling year-over-year.
